responses.correspondence:
To Brigadier-General Charles Tenley, 

The first court of the Commonwealth of Australia has occurred and the outcomes are as follows: leading the court is Premier Eve Lockridge, Scottish. She had been tending to Australia while kindred could not enter using mortal connections, and is a Carthian but was once Invictus. I spoke with the Carthian who I heard played a large role in her conversion, an English woman named Renee, but she was aggressive and avoidant when I brought it up- this may be an exploitative sore point. 

The Harpy (Secretary of the Premier) is an American Gangrel called Brandy. She is a Crone, practically a witch, and very charismatic and influential- perhaps even moreso than the Premier. The Protector of the Court is an Irish Carthian Gangrel called Dr Kane. She is quite intimidating. On terms of stability I believe such a young court could be fractured. 

The Lancea Sanctum here have a Gangrel Paladin called Kirk Grimm who is very loyal to the church but naive to the ways of the Gangrel. Brigadier, I believe if I can teach him the ways of our clan I could gain his trust and gain us an ally. Our inquisitor is an English man named Conrad, who believes he has heard of you. All good things, Brigadier, I assure you! 
They are all of the "Westminster Creed" save for our American Bishop and Confessor Edna Judge. I admit I don't know what creed we follow- would it be the Tenley Creed? I have not answered any questions regarding this. 

I trust the pack is not acting foolishly in my absence.

Sincerely,
Tegan Groves

responses.correspondence:
Dear Arthur,

Thank you for putting me in contact with your cousin. I'm sorry I couldn't bring you here, but I'm grateful that somebody is there to keep an eye on Ellen for me while I serve Heavenly Father on the opposite end of the world. The Sanctified here in this city are certainly... Something. I've worked my fair share with protestants of all breeds but they certainly are different when they come directly from their homeland. The Orthodox woman is lovely, I think we get along.

Anyhow. The praxis structure of this place is entirely illegitimate. Some Carthian Experiment. If I were born yesterday I'd say Miss Lockridge seems to think she has everybody fooled with her niceties but I very much wasn't and I don't think she was either. It's a shame, really, all of those skills in talking away wasted for not being up at the pulpit. She will realise she is damned sooner or later. Whether we can speed her to that realisation remains to be seen.

Your friend in Faith,
E. J. Judge
responses.project_1_description:
Project Name: Dearly Beloved (i)

Project description: 
Edna arrives at Woronora cemetery under the cover of night, veiled as any proper mourning wife should be, but still obfuscating reflexively so that any other visitors to the cemetery might have their attention simply slide off her.

This is her first time in this cemetery; her ghoul Brayden has informed her that Lehi has finally been reinterred here. She takes her time finding him tonight, scrap of paper with his lot information balled in her fist. In each new city she familiarises herself with her late husband's new home and neighbours, picking her way through the rows on the lawns and inspecting the rosebushes. 

When you spend your time with so many of those who should be long since dead the stories that these headstones and plaques can - on occasion - tell a story of the present rather than one of the past. With so many of these newly met kindred coming from all over and very few originally from this city, she doubts that she will find anything. But she looks all the same. Reads each name on the Jacaranda lawn. Greets them each by name, sending a prayer to Heavenly Father for every one of their damned souls.

When she arrives at Lehi, she kneels at the foot of his grave and gives her testimony to him, 

"I know that Heavenly Father lives and loves His children, that Jesus Christ is the Savior and Redeemer of the kine. I believe Joseph Smith is a prophet who restored the church. I believe that The Book of Mormon is true. The church of the kine is led by a living prophet, although Heavenly Father has not gifted him full understanding of the world as it is, and I know this is to protect the flock."

After this, she simply sits and chats a while in the quiet. Giving her testimony to Lehi always helps her feel more grounded, and it was such a long journey to Sydney without him. She jokes that she has no housekeeping to do because his home is so tidy and that she imagines there will be more of a mess as he begins to settle in. She complains of the traffic and the miserable weather. Tells him that last she spoke to Arthur, Ellen was in good health. She never tells him that she has left the church. She doesn't stray in to kindred business tonight - as a rule she tries not to. This is not a "life" that he was destined to know, and talking to him about the mundanities around her makes her feel less monstrous, at least for a time.

Characters involved: Edna Judge

Skills and Powers used: obfuscate 1 + investigation

Desired outcome: any leads on Sydney based kindred (if any), otherwise this is just part of her trying to work on her humanity that I (Arlo) plan to do regularly

XP Spent: None

responses.travel:
Brandy travels to and from Court quietly but with a touch of personal style, usually in an expensive black car that draws just enough attention to suit her tastes without revealing too much about her movements. She avoids predictable routes, often arriving early or lingering late rather than moving with the main crowd, keeping to the edges where she can observe before entering.

Her precautions are practical rather than paranoid. She keeps her travel simple, avoids bringing unnecessary mortal attention, and ensures no one follows her to or from Court. Years of surviving both her sire and the wild have taught her that safety often lies in patience, awareness, and choosing exactly when to be seen.

responses.correspondence:
Marielle, my sweet sister,

Sydney’s court has finally begun to show its true colours, and I figured you’d enjoy hearing how the dance is shaping up over here. The city’s new court is polished enough on the surface — smiles, pleasantries, all the usual little performances — but it doesn’t take long to notice where the seams are stitched. 

I’ll say this kindly: the Crones here have big shoes to fill. There’s promise in the coven, certainly… though one in particular — the Keeper — gives me the absolute willies. You know I trust my instincts about such things.

The Primogen seats are held by Rene St. Dominique of the Invictus and Conrad Sondergaard of the Lancea. The Invictus have been polite enough, but quiet and colder than a snake in winter. Meanwhile the Lancea have already started making their presence known — though perhaps not in the way they intended. Juicy little bit of gossip for you: they’ve apparently snubbed the Premier by never properly introducing themselves. For a covenant so fond of propriety, that’s a mighty curious oversight. There’s also talk that the Lancea may be making a play for the Port, which I’ll be keeping a close eye on.

The Carthians, on the other hand, are standing rather tall at the moment. Premier Eve Lockheart and a few other names keep rising to the top — Doc, Mac, and Einar. This Doc in particular has caught my attention. I can’t yet decide if they are clever, dangerous, or simply enjoying the sound of their own reputation — but I aim to find out.

As for me, the Premier has handed me the delicate duty of Socialite. Someone has to keep the stories straight and the reputations tidy — and I suspect I’ll be hearing plenty worth remembering before long.

Do be a dear and sniff around the courts back in Baton Rouge for me. If any of these names — Lockheart, Sondergaard, St. Dominique, or that curious Doctor — carry a history worth knowing, I’d love to hear it. You always did have the better ear for juicy gossip, and Lord knows a girl can’t do this job properly without good intelligence.

I miss you something fierce, sugar. Nights like these I could use your voice in my ear reminding me which smiles to trust and which ones hide teeth.

All my love,

Brandy

responses.correspondence:
Dearest H,

Court is one big power-play at the moment. Everyone is sniffing each other's butts trying to figure out who to show their belly to and who to snarl at.

The Carthians are running the joint at the moment, but they've got an Invictus and Lance Primogen and one of ours as the Harpy so it's a power-share right out of the gate. I know you said to watch the Invictus but they seem low on numbers at the moment.

I've caught up with all of the other Crones and made their acquaintance. Our elected Mother is very old country, very 'I need to remix Kate Bush's "Babooshka" and give it to her as a present.' Oh, and Jack Fallow is in town! Good to have an industry connection handy for the phone numbers I might not have, right?

H, there was a Nosferatu who got well and truly up in my face about 'what happened in Thailand'. I've lost count of the number of times I've been there in the last 20 years on your dime...but he might have meant the Koh Samui gig. We got a lot of good mojo out of that but you never told me who the losing side was. If you can do a little digging to see which players were in town during that game it'd be great. Mel's tried and she's at a loss, but she's also kinda new. 

Love ya!

 - C

responses.travel: Maggie and Mac make a point of visibly catching public transport to and from Court. Sometimes trains, sometimes buses- depending where Court is held. They always get on from a different station or stop, and on the way home exit from a different station than they got on. The before and after is where the field-craft comes in; they double back, go down blind alleys and cut through abandoned lots. If they need to, they call in minor favours for a Carthian Limo (aka a plain white van with enough bodies in it to make sneaking in with Obfuscate too difficult to be worthwhile).
responses.feeding_description:
Mac can’t feed by seduction or trickery like most Vampires; the Norvegi curse is a genuine problem. He’ll be looking for abattoirs and hospitals where he can clean up spilled blood in large quantities, or sometimes pounds where animals are due to be put down and no-one will be too interested if they die suddenly the night before. He’s careful, patient and practical about feeding; he avoids running on empty if he can so he’s never feeding out of desperation (but of course best laid plans, etc). 

If everything goes completely to shit, he bails out and relies on Carthian favours to get ahold of blood bags or tap into someone’s Herd. 

Dex/Wits/Composure + Stealth (+ Obfuscate if he’s really up against it, is worried about detection and can’t throw time at the problem).
responses.trust:
Doc Maggie, and it’s not close. 

After her it’s probably René Meyer, but that might be less trust than just knowing what to expect.
responses.harm: Kirk Grimm is at the top of the list, but the list is long. Kirk has put the Lance near the top, but the Invictus are all on there too, and at least 2 Carthians (Yusuf and Eve) qualify for ‘hamper’ just because of their Invictus connections.
responses.game_recount:
-Got to talk a bit with the other Carthians as a group. Pretty militant bunch as these things go; there’s some promise there. 
-Ballsack turning up here is pretty fucking weird, and him having that girl on a leash is beyond coincidence. Gotta be some fucked up angle there but I’m fucked if I can work it out. 
-That murderous motherfucker Grimm is here too. He’s playing the friendly dog but I’ve seen his rabid eyes. That dog is gonna have to be out down sooner than later. Hopefully he gives us an excuse. The Lance fancy boy he’s protecting a honest-to-bullshit English pig too. Thinks he’s fucking Columbo but couldn’t get himself fed before fucking Court. 
-Met most of the Crone. They’re as weird and fucking crazy as usual but they seem keen to be friendly (suspicious) and are the only obvious potential allies the Movement has. Their level of infiltration of mortal groups is concerning. 
-Maggie got made Protector (always likes being top dog) and I somehow caught a stray acknowledgment. This Premier still thinks like a fucking Invictus. If she can’t get over that we’ll have to crowbar it out of her. Maybe literally.
responses.rp_shoutout: Kirk (Phil) was really nailing the ‘best of enemies’ vibe. Fantastically ‘loud’ and obnoxious.
responses.correspondence:
Karoline,

Ballsack's here. Nosferatu prick from Belfast. Has a girl we transported once. Kindred now. Keeps her on a leash—pale thing, fucked up, don’t know shit. Beyond coincidence. Can't see the angle yet but there has to be one.

Grimm too. Lance attack dog that tore me up back in Belfast. Playing friendly now but I remember those eyes when they're rabid. Needs putting down. He's got company—English Lance type, plays detective; couldn't feed himself before Court started. Arrogant little shit. 

Met the local Carthians. Small crew. Militant. Promise there.

Circle's weird as usual. Keen to be friendly—suspicious—but only obvious allies. Their mortal reach is concerning. Deep infiltration.

Maggie's Protector of Sydney now. Caught a stray acknowledgment from their Premier in the announcement. She thinks like Invictus. Thinks praxis means throne. If she can't learn different we'll crowbar it out of her.

That's the shape of it. New country same shit.

—Mac
responses.project_1_description:
Project Name: Building Bridges
Project description: Mac needs mortal eyes. Transport first—docks, depots, union pubs. The men who move things. He knows the rhythm: show up, listen, buy rounds, be useful. Let them trust the quiet older fella who worked docks back home. Street second—gangs, homeless, kids in doorways who see everything. He walks their edges, pays in favours, leaves them alone when they need it. When something moves through the dark that shouldn't, they'll remember who to tell..
Characters involved: Mac
Skills and Powers used: Wits + Empathy to read who's reliable and who's a liability; maybe some Investigation to dig up the right people. 
Desired outcome: Establish Contacts (Transport) 1 dot—reliable mortal eyes in docks, depots, and union halls. Establish Allies (Street) 1 dot—street gangs, the homeless, the disenfranchised. 
XP Spent: 1 XP for Contacts (Transport), 1 XP for Allies (Street)
responses.project_2_description:
Project Name: Walking the Beat
Project description: Maggie wants to be seen. Mac wants to see what's there. They'll spend nights patrolling the Port and Harbour together—conspicuous, unmistakable, playing the role of Carthian heavies staking out turf. Let the Invictus and Lance types see them. Let them wonder. Let them report back. While they're at it, they'll note traffic patterns, kindred movement, which shadows have eyes and which are just shadows. The information matters, but the image matters more: two old Fenian troubleshooters, thick as thieves, unsubtle as a brick through a window.
Characters involved: Mac, Maggie (Doctor Kane)
Skills and Powers used: Wits + Composure for observation, Presence + Intimidation for the performance of it, maybe some Streetwise
Desired outcome: Establish a visible Carthian presence in the Port and the Harbour, gather basic intelligence on local kindred activity there, send a message
XP Spent: None
responses.project_3_description:
Project Name: The Real Patrol
Project description: The show patrol with Maggie was for them. This one's for him. Mac shaves everything—hair, beard, the lot—knowing it'll grow back tomorrow but needing the ritual of it. Plain clothes. Different walk. Different man. He slips into the Second City with Obfuscate snuffing out the predator beneath his skin, just another tired soul on night streets. He watches. He listens. He maps. Kindred pass and he fades into doorways, into crowds, into nothing. No confrontations. No statements. Just eyes and memory, building a picture of how this city actually moves when it thinks no one's looking.
Characters involved: Mac
Skills and Powers used: Wits + Stealth for moving unseen, Wits + Investigation for gathering intel, Obfuscate (Face in the Crowd) to suppress predator's aura and blend, Composure to stay calm when danger passes close
Desired outcome: Detailed intelligence on Second City kindred activity—territory lines, habitual routes, gathering spots, weaknesses—without anyone knowing he was there
XP Spent: None
responses.project_4_description:
Project Name: Dog-Watching
Project description: Kirk Grimm is here, playing friendly, pretending the past is past. Mac doesn't believe it for a second. But the one holding Grimm's leash is Mekhet too...so this needs extra care. No close work. No Obfuscate brushing against Obfuscate in the dark. Just distance, patience, and the kind of watching Mac learned young: from across streets, through windows, via patterns noticed and discarded details collected. Where does Grimm go when he thinks no one's looking? Who does he meet? What does he do when the friendly dog mask slips? Mac will find out, even if it takes all night, every night, until he knows exactly where the knife goes when the time comes.
Characters involved: Mac, Kirk Grimm, possibly Conrad
Skills and Powers used: Wits + Stealth for long-range observation, Wits + Investigation for pattern recognition, Obfuscste 1 to minimise chance of detection. Auspex if Mac pushes his awareness (carefully, so carefully) to catch details normal eyes would miss
Desired outcome: Understand Grimm's current role, connections, and movements in Sydney. Identify weaknesses, patterns, and whether he's building toward something. All without Grimm or his patron knowing they're being watched.
XP Spent: None
responses.contact_actions_raw:
Contact/Allies/Retainer: Contacts 1 (Street)
Project description: Mac puts word through his street connections—gang kids, homeless, doorway sleepers—asking what they've seen that doesn't fit. People acting wrong. Blood left where blood shouldn't be. Faces that show up only at night, same time, same place, like clockwork. Disappearances that don't make the news but get whispered about in hostels and squat houses. He's not asking them to hunt monsters. Just to remember what catches their eye, what makes their skin prickle, what they'd normally tell themselves they imagined. The street sees everything. Most of it gets ignored. He needs the parts that shouldn't be there.
Desired outcome: Intelligence on possible Kindred activity in street-level Sydney, with particular attention to feeding patterns, territory markers, or breaches of the Masquerade that mortal eyes have noticed and might talk about.

Contact/Allies/Retainer: Contacts 1 (Underworld)
Project description: Through his existing underworld contacts—the ones who deal in stolen goods, unlicensed guns, favours for cash—Mac asks quieter questions. Who's running things now? Which names keep coming up in whispers from Sydney's criminal half-light? Not the street-level soldiers, the ones with actual weight. The importers, the money men, the people who settle disputes because everyone's afraid to tell them no. He's not looking to make trouble. Just to know whose toes he'd be stepping on if trouble finds him anyway. The underworld has its own maps. He needs to see them.
Desired outcome: A clear picture of the power structure in Sydney's organised crime—who holds territory, who controls major pipelines (drugs, guns, stolen goods), and which names carry real weight when deals go bad.

responses.travel:
Jack likes to blend into crowds, predatory aura fully off, with OBS 1 (Face in the Crowd). 
He parks his car on a side street near a train station several stops away from Court and takes public transport. This way, he can people watch -- observing Kine go about their business, oblivious to the secret world around them. 
Before disembarking, he will use his Beast's Hackles (Auspex 1) to check for threats, such as hidden spying kindred or other supernaturals to make sure he isn't being followed. If he is, then he goes to the next stop to try to lose them and circle back. Otherwise, he gets out and leaves the station.
Once unobserved on a quiet side street, he uses his Celerity to zip quickly to Court or back to his car. 
Before driving (his Tesla 3)  back to his haven, Jack raises his Beast's Hackles one last time to detect threats, he can then enjoy a relaxed drive, moon roof open, to reflect on the events of Court before making his way back to the area where he has his haven. He then uses Celerity again to close distance close to his haven before quietly slipping in to wind down before sunrise.
responses.feeding_description:
Jack always uses Face in the Crowd (OBS 1), predatory aura fully off, when moving amongst the Kine. 

Jack feeds primarily on members of his cult (The Moulding Room). Jack had already established virtual contact with Kine associated with the Room located in Sydney before coming, identifying any regular 'Visitors' (leading to his 3-dot Ally in High Society), and resulting in his 5-dot Cult standing and corresponding herd active upon arrival in Sydney. 

These Kine include a number of 'Watchers' scattered around the city, who have been inadvertently (for the most part) feeding the Eye behind the Lens. Jack is in the process of systematically reaching out to meet with these, both to keep tabs (including gathering information about their secrets with Uncanny Perception (Auspex **) and also to feed. 

Jack always feeds in a subtle way when they are alone and/or in a secluded spot (whether at their residence, in the bathroom of an internet cafe, or dark shadows of a park), allowing the Kiss to run its course, causing the Kine to swoon and experience euphoria, followed by confusion and lack of clarity about what actually happened. A simple double kiss on each cheek (how European of him...) can lead easily into the Kiss. Catching them at the end, as they literally swoon, with an "Oh, my, are you all right?" provides cover, letting them think they suddenly got light-headed for no apparent reaason...

Jack always licks their wounds to leave no trace of the incident. 

Jack relies on his Medicine (**) skill to ensure that he does not overfeed and kill his prey, including to ascertain whether they might be in a weakened physical state such that even light feeding would be unwise.

responses.travel: Maggie and Mac make a point of visibly catching public transport to and from Court. Sometimes trains, sometimes buses- depending where Court is held. They always get on from a different station or stop, and on the way home exit from a different station than they got on. The before and after is where the field-craft comes in; they double back, go down blind alleys and cut through abandoned lots. If they need to, they call in minor favours for a Carthian Limo (aka a plain white van with enough bodies in it to make sneaking in with Obfuscate too difficult to be worthwhile).
responses.feeding_description: Maggie finds a pub or a bar with good music and bad lighting, finds someone who looks like they could use a bit of luck, and buys the first round. By the end of the night they've had a grand, if blurry time, she's had what she came for, and everyone walks out under their own steam. Using Manipulation + Empathy
responses.harm: Kirk Grimm, and the Lance Primogen.
responses.game_recount:
- Walked the Court, gauging responses to Carthian principles of rule aka democracy for all. Some interest from younger Crone.
- Encountered an old enemy, Kirk Grimm. Did not allow Mac to murder him on sight. He's protecting the Lance Primogen, which is unsurprising but irritating. Will have to get rid of both. 
- Possibly agreed to attend a party run by one of the Crone who was very enthusiastic about existing. Suspicious. 
- Met (re-met?) a lass who we'd last encountered as a human, leashed to Sack. Further information required. 
- Premier appointed me Protector of Sydney. Just what I always wanted, another bloody Court position. The power will be useful though...
responses.rp_shoutout: Bill Cohen and Phil G, for sure.
responses.correspondence:
Comrade- 

Sydney Court may be the newest in the business, but the smell is the same- old blood, older lies, and everyone dancing around, pretending the rot isn't in the bones. Walked the room anyway and floated the Movement's line- might have caught the interest of some of the younger Crone. Kirk Grimm crawled out of whatever hole he's been hiding in for forty years, following the Lance Primogen around like the great slavering dog he is. Didn't let Mac solve that particular problem on sight, which I might come to regret. I've been named Protector by that Premier- you know, the young one. Twenty years and a hundred thousand miles and I'm back in the muck of Court politics. What am I thinking? 

Doc
responses.project_1_description:
Project Name: Walking the Beat
Project Description: Mac and I are making ourselves visible, this month around the Port and the Harbour. We'll spend a few nights walking around, watching who moves through the area, and letting anyone who's paying attention know exactly who's out there. The point's half reconnaissance and half theatre- two old troubleshooters from the Movement walking the city so that the various flavours of fascists know that we're here, and we're paying attention. 
Characters involved: Doc and Mac
Skills and Powers used: Presence + Intimidation for the show, Intelligence + Composure for the observation. 
Desired outcome: Conduct a patrol of the Port and the Harbour while observing Kindred activity, and making Doc and Mac's presence known. 
XP Spent: None.
responses.project_2_description:
Project Name: A Friendly Drink
Project description: I find a bar with decent noise and buy someone the sort of night they’ll remember fondly and vaguely. Nothing dramatic—just conversation, a few rounds, and a quiet bite before they stumble home thinking they met a generous stranger. It keeps the hunger down and the Masquerade intact, which suits me fine.
Characters involved: Doc
Skills and Powers used: Manipulation + Empathy, Persuasion if need be
Desired outcome: Feed safely, without breaching the Masquerade, and not arriving to Court hangry. 
XP Spent: None
responses.project_3_description:
Project Name: Street Medicine
Project description: There’s always people falling through the cracks in a city this size, so I’m volunteering a few nights at a free clinic around the Second City. Patch people up, listen to their problems, make it clear someone actually gives a damn whether they live or die. A little care goes a long way toward changing the mood of a place.
Characters involved: Doc
Skills and Powers used: Medicine, Empathy, Professional training (Combat medic) 
Desired Outcome: increase the Ambience of the Second City 
XP Spent: None
responses.project_4_description:
Project Name: Direct Line
Project description: Being Protector of Sydney means someone has to know who to call when the city’s on fire—or when a Masquerade breach goes sideways. I’m setting up a pager number that can take calls or texts from ordinary mobiles, and distributing it through trusted Carthian contacts so other Kindred know exactly where to reach me. No middlemen, no surprises—if they need me, it’s on my terms.
Characters involved: Doc, various Carthian connections
Skills and Powers used: Presence + Politics, City Status. 
Desired Outcome: Establish a secure, controlled line of contact for emergencies and Masquerade breaches
XP Spent: None
responses.contact_actions_raw:
Contacts 2 (Medical) 
Project Name: Unusual Bloodwork
Project description: I still know people in the hospital system, the sort who notice when the numbers stop making sense. I’m asking them to keep an eye out for oddities—spikes in blood bank draws, cases of unexplained anaemia, anything that looks like someone’s been drinking too deep from the locals. If someone in Sydney’s getting sloppy with their feeding, I’d like to know where before it turns into a Masquerade problem.
Characters involved: Doc
Desired outcome: Identify potential overfeeding incidents or feeding hotspots across Sydney
XP Spent: None

Contacts 2 (Politics) 
Project Name: What the Academy Needs
Project description: I’m leaning on my political contacts to get a clearer picture of what the Academy district actually needs—funding gaps, infrastructure repairs, community services, the usual list of things councils promise and forget. If someone’s supposed to be running that part of the city, they’ll want the information sooner or later. I’d prefer to be the one holding it when they come asking.
Characters involved: Doc
Desired outcome: Gather actionable information about infrastructure and community needs in the Academy region
XP Spent: None
responses.xp_spend: None this month
responses.aspirations:
Short term: Secure my position as Protector, and make sure I'm the one that people call when the Masquerade goes sideways- on my terms, always. 

Medium term: Keep an eye on the younger Crone and even any young and rebellious Invictus or Lance, see if any of them could be convinced to join the Movement. 

Long term: Stop anyone turning Sydney into another Belfast - nobody gets to retain power for long. Limit the monsters who think they can run the city like a playground.

responses.rp_shoutout: Alice (played by Katherine) kept Anichka company most of the night, and was constantly amicable and quick witted. Rene (played by Luca) was startled to see Anichka due to their history, but was quick on the uptake and trying to understand and manipulate current politics
responses.correspondence:
Dear Sire,

I am in Sydney. It is very hot and I do not like it, but the trees are nice. Fragrant. I think you would like it here, the shadows are long and full of secrets.
Our siblings have named me Hierophant, and I do not like it. But if this is the path I am laid forward, then I will submit to this duty.
I am surrounded by people I have given prophecy to, and this worries me. It probably means there is a bigger vision around it, and means I am part of it. I do not like those visions.
I have not been in a court as long as this in a while. I am becoming aware of the things I lack when I talk to people, or see them, or listen to them. There will be fast and important lessons to learn, and heavy consequences if I do not learn them fast enough.

I have faith. I am here for a reason, and I am finding it. The path forward is coming to us, and we will be ready.

In service to the Gods, I remain
Anichka.

responses.project_1_description:
Project Name: Wisdom of the Witch Hazel
Project description: Charles would like to know more about the Lance Inquisitor that has become one of the high figures at court. And what better way to learn than to understand the body? Charles makes a scaled down clay sculpture of Sister Hazel, working upwards from a wire armature of bone, to muscle, to flesh, and eventually clothing. Each layer is anatomically correct as Charles lets his intuition and cruac guide the completion of artwork. The final glaze has vitae mixed into it, with other ritualistic flourishes as Charles finishes and fires the doll in his kiln. 
Characters involved: Just Charles 
Skills and Powers used: Cruac Ritual "Wisdom of the Soul", Crafts (Sculpting), Occult? 
Desired outcome: Learn the humanity rating and maybe mask or dirge of Marni's character Sister Hazel. Have a clay doll of Sister Hazel sitting in a cabinet for later use.

Project Name: The Contagion (pt.1)
Project description: Inspired by Annichka's visions of the blood seeping through the city in different mirrored levels, Charles will be working on a large-scale sculpture to be displayed.... somewhere appropriate. It is a sculpture depicting the iconic Sydney skyline and major landmarks on either side of a smooth plane in the middle with one inverted. Some sections of the sculpture are moulded from transparent heat-form plastic so that the web of tubing can be seen underneath while others are ceramic. Connecting the two halves of the sculpture is a web of tubing like veins or capillaries. Eventually, Charles intends to fill some of the sculpture with blood and animate the vitae to move between the two visions of the city.  
Characters involved: Just Charles 
Skills and Powers used: Crafts (Sculpting), Expression? Occult?
Desired outcome: Begin working on this big sculpture.

responses.correspondence:
To my Comrades in Anti-Obstructionism

I have arrived safely, or at least as safe as one can be under these circumstances. Surprisingly, and suspiciously, there's a number of faces I recognize attending court. Already I can see numerous obstacles in cementing Sydney as a hands-off sort of city, but I we were never expecting this to be easy. Doesn't help that our Premier is barley even not a mortal themselves, a fledgling of a few years- their connection to mortality is only going to rouse more issues... Oh well. I at least have some positive leads to follow.

~R
responses.project_1_description:
Project Name: Corpse Spotting

Project description: The first court, the first true Kindred foothold in Sydney- surely there will be some folks who haven't prepared their defenses yet. This is the best time to get to know where people are setting up, and how they're doing it. 

Rene is going to sit outside the court, after leaving a little early. There she will obfuscate and sit in stealth- though not too fussed if people see her. She will watch how people leave court, what direction they go, what vehicles they drive- and their number-plates, who they leave with (If there's any ghouls), as well as any other pertinent details. Particular attention will be paid towards Conrad, Reed Justice, Keeper, and Brandy LaRoux.

She's never been a great investigator, but perhaps she'll be lucky enough to pick up some details.

Characters involved: Rene

Skills and Powers used: Obfuscate, Investigation, Hold My Beer (To give her Investigation)

Desired outcome: Broad information about the Kindred leaving court, with particular attention to those names mentioned.

Using Spirits Touch to get some information out of the wallet, alongside the general information within such as license, credit card information, and any other general oddities they store. The question that she'll ask is 'What is an impactful purchase made with this wallet recently'

Project description: Over the course of my unlife, I realize that I try too hard. Instead of flitting about trying to get things done, I should just relax a bit and let things come to me... I've a habit of stumbling into things. So I'm going to do some wandering around, and see what falls in my way. No need to make a hubbub about it.

Rene hopes to tune in to the Cacophony now that she's been in the city for a little while, getting the lay of the land.

Characters involved: Rene

Skills and Powers used: Cacophony Savvy + Streetwise (Rumors)

responses.project_4_description:
Project Name: Tune in to 185.3 - Red Night Radio

Project description: "Hello listeners, wonderful for you to join me in this cool summer's night. I'm your Host, Ren- and today we'll be talking about the recent scares folks have had along the shores of Sydney. I'm taking callers, so if you've got anything to say- you can say it right to me..."

With the beginnings of her radio show Rene hopes to give mortal society a bit of a fighting chance against the irrepressible influence of Kindred. This is the way she's using her influence to reduce Kindred's hold on Sydney and the various territories they're building- telling people of an upturn of muggings and violence in the dark, that they should stick together and to lighter areas, among other safety tips. Nothing masquerade breaking, but precautions against crime and against Kindred tend to have a lot of overlap. Perhaps next time she'll be able to do something about the deep-seeded connections Kindred have in mortal associations. A bit of attention payed towards the Port, perhaps she can keep her touchstone out of harms way...

Characters involved: Rene

Skills and Powers used: Turn of Phrase + Manipulation + Persuasion, Mortal Status (Media)

Desired outcome: To prepare the mortal collective against the oncoming tide of Vampires, trying to make her unlife easier in the long run... Unprepared mortals make good ghouls after all. And, perhaps- some late-night callers might have some tidbits of information to give.

XP Spent: Zero

responses.game_recount:
Dear Marquis Elious, 

Tonight was the first night of Court under the regime. At present a Carthian named Eve is the Premier of Sydney, she has a curious experiment in mind. I admire her conviction, but believe that her goals are ultimately doomed. 

The Invictus that have arrived in this city are a curious group to be sure. There is a Knight from the Order of St Martin here. Knight Ballsack is a horrible sight to behold, a pitiful creature in some ways but his Oaths are iron much like his fighting prowess I’m told. I met Justice Reed as well, seems a capable, cunning individual one to guide for sure. 

There is also a Lady Lutica who appears to be interested in building a library of occult knowledge and a new Lord named Wan. A quiet type who I will watch. 

I spent much of the night conversing with Eve and giving her advice, as a plan to like a father offers advice to a child who has no plan to listen, I don’t plan on stepping in until things are dire. 

You may have a laugh over the fact that the Circle had no witch or warlock to discuss matters with, so I imposed on them a meeting to choose a leader who I could be civil with. 

The Second Estate have expressed their support of us as ever we would show to them, we provided them with some influence to cover territory to feed in the city as we did for the Prince, not the Carthians mind you and even some cursory support for the Circle to set a precedent that we are a base of power. 

I will keep you abreast of all future matters my liege. 

Yours in service, Rene.

- Met up with Eve, Rene, and the other Carthians to discuss our plans for the city.
- Saw Cyrus Reynolds, reminisced about old times, and started planning a party for the city.
- Met a number of new kindred - notably Sister Hazel, Tegan Groves, and Reed Justice, as well as Keeper, Ivana, and Ballsack.
- Noticed Eve was missing. Went searching with Maggie and MacHeath. Discovered a door in the building that was 'magically' sealed. Attempted to kick it in to no avail, and a short time later Eve emerged, along with most of the Invictus.
- Had a "serious chat" with Eve about how difficult it is to keep her alive if she is going to lock herself in a room with a bunch of strange Kindred without even telling us.

responses.correspondence:
Lord X - Sire to Premier Eve Lockridge

Sydney is bustling. There are more Kindred here than I had imagined, and far more Lance than I would prefer. It seems that the tensions of London have followed us all the way to the Southern Continent. Their ideology is immovable, and I do not doubt that they will either attempt to seize power, or at the very least remove Eve by one means or another.

Your Childe is competent in many ways, but I feel she does not fully appreciate the precariousness of her, and by extension our situation here. I took my eyes off her for only a short time and found that she had disappeared - allowing herself to be locked in a room with half a dozen Kindred that she did not know, and who are not allies to her cause. It will be a miracle if she survives the first six months of this Praxis.

I understand, and will stick to the terms of our agreement. I will keep her alive as best as I am able, I will head off threats to her Praxis and her life wherever I find them - but I cannot be held responsible for what happens if she is going to pull shit like that.

I can say that at least for now, it would seem that your investment is in safe hands. There are not as yet too many stand outs within the Kindred community that I can point to as potential "leaders", but everybody here is keen to make a life for themself, which means cultivating a safe space for Kindred.

That is a hell of a sentence.

responses.travel:
I travel under Obs 1/4, probably on public transport/Uber.
Mortal followers Allies and my resources will be used to organise a limousine transport between my liar in North Sydney and the court location. There will be significant mortal security appropriate to a UHNWI or VVIP transferred - two security vehicles, armoured security limo, armed body-guards with earpieces. All the theatrics. I will not be in it. Perhaps if we take attendants and consorts to court one of them will hitch the ride. In reality I will be outside the court early, in deep obfuscation, taking some interest in watching others arrive, up to the point my limo finally arrives fashionably late (at which point I join it, as the door opens at the red carpet).
responses.feeding_description:
Typically I feed using my secret cult. The cult of Mammon is a high-status mystery cult specifically targeting wealth and the greedy banking elite. By the time they reach the highest levels of the organisation they are introduced to Mammon- himself. At this level they share the resources and gain full initiation into the cult. They will never see me. Dread Presence, Grand Delusion, Illusory terrain all maintain elaborate misdirection theatrics. Part of the offering ritual is blindfolds, ecstasy and offering. They present themselves for feeding without even knowing that they are being feed upon. They think they are being blessed by the demon-lord Mammon in a cosmic quest for riches and power. Illusions support that belief. They see only shadows in an opulent room, and smouldering hoof-prints burned into carpet. They smell only brimstone, cash and burnt plastic. They hear distant hell-moan, chants from outside rooms and a low thrum. They face away for the "Kiss". I take only 2 vitae from each to preserve the illusion. I drink through a cannula - to avoid touching them

My bane prevents me from being in the company of virtuous mortals. These greedy, murderous cunts are perfect blood-bags. The best part, they will actually murder each other for my favour to advance. By the time they feed upon they are so morally compromised that they can't back out - veiled threats of blackmail - "so deeply stepped into a river of blood that to go over is easier than to turn back" i.e. "if the photos existed I cant imagine whom they'd interest".

HERD (5) OR
Presence (4) + Intimidation (4) (+1 veiled threats) for new top level members to replace those murdered by rivals (infrequent)
responses.trust: this is interesting question. Yusuf is interested in the Brandy Harpy but this is not trust but entangled fascination. the character I trust so far the most would be 'Rene' of the Carthians. This neonate firebrand is perhaps misguided, but Rene has already shared solid information and network building within the Covenant. Yusuf perceives this as politically naive, but a clear demonstration of Carthian values. Therefore, Rene is immediately a contact of interest within the Carthians. My early motivation in court business is to help setup common resource pool for the movement. It has been made known to me that the Mac/Doc block is a solid foundational capacity of the movement, but Rene is the character I 'trust' the most since their motivations and actions apparently align transparently. Basically, in the treacherous court body-politic I trust that Rene will be the least guarded. This makes Rene a valuable comrade and also perhaps someone who needs protecting from themselves. Yusuf is a trader of secrets in a covenant that values transparency. My secrets are held for collective protection. Rene reminds me of an old colleague, Solomon Clay, from the Bayou Carthians - similarly open-handed and final-dead as a result of it.
responses.harm:
No one I see yet.

I am not placed to be able to act against Invictus interests. Im not rabidly anti-Invictus in any case. I just hate certain tendencies of the invictus to commoditise and ritualise humiliation and enslavement. When that comes up, it will change.

I may have reason to contest with characters for The Shore regency. But I am a latecomer and Ill need details before I can take effective blocking actions
responses.game_recount:
I was not in attendance at the game due to a health issue. However, my spies tried to gather as much intel as possible on the Harpy Crone, and her struggles with her sudden status. I have an interest to protect her and her sudden elevation in the court seems like a setup - so it hastened my in-character movement from Bayou to Sydney under urgent circumstances. The official mission of setting up a Mammon-franchise in Sydney as an offer of support to the surprising Carthian Praxis is the superficial cover-story for my movement.

Having arrived in Sydney too late for primary court, but needing to assess the lay-of the land (at least Covenant wise). I gently reached out to intro to the covenant, but there have been no takers save for Rene - who shares information too freely.

responses.project_1_description:
Project Name: Follow the Money

Project Description: Reed Justice has been building mortal financial infrastructure in the Shore through a company called Palleon International — ore refining, AI integration, online security, and who knows what else. Before the Movement makes any decisions about the Shore, I need to know exactly what we're dealing with. I'll use my Finance contacts to pull threads on Palleon International — corporate filings, business relationships, subcontractors, key personnel — and my Obfuscate to physically surveil his mortal assets in the Shore without being detected. I'm not disrupting anything. I'm building a picture. Mostly Palleon, but also looking for other touches that seem to indicate kindred influence on Finance sphere.

Characters Involved: Just me

Skills and Powers Used: Wits + Finance (forensic financial analysis via contacts), Intelligence + Investigation (tracing corporate structures), Obfuscate 4 (physical surveillance of key sites and mortal agents without detection)

Desired Outcome: Build a detailed intelligence picture of Reed's mortal operations in the Shore — key personnel, business fronts, financial flows, and the true scope of Palleon International's footprint in the territory.

XP Spent: None
responses.project_2_description:
Project Name: Due Diligence

Project Description: Palleon International does ore refining among other things. Refining operations mean environmental regulations, workplace safety requirements, union labour, government contracts, and tax obligations. Every mortal operation has pressure points — especially one expanding aggressively. I'm going to use my Finance contacts to quietly dig into whether Palleon has any regulatory skeletons: environmental violations, workplace safety complaints, underpaid or mistreated workers, dodgy tax arrangements, contracts that don't hold up to scrutiny. I'm looking for anything that could be leveraged, disrupted, or simply reported to the right mortal authorities at the right time. I'll be careful not to tip anyone off that someone is looking.

Characters Involved: Just me

Skills and Powers Used: Intelligence + Finance (forensic analysis via contacts), Manipulation + Subterfuge (asking the right questions through intermediaries without revealing intent)

Desired Outcome: Identify specific vulnerabilities in Reed's mortal operations — regulatory exposure, labour grievances, tax irregularities, environmental issues — anything that represents a friction point that could be exploited or used as leverage.

XP Spent: None
responses.project_3_description:
Project Name: Closed for Business

Project Description: The Invictus are small in Sydney, but Reed Justice may be building financial influence in the Shore and the Movement hasn't had a chance to discuss what we want that territory to look like. Until the Carthian committee has reached consensus on the Shore, I don't think any covenant should be consolidating power there unopposed. I'm invoking Picket Line to establish a Carthian presence that disrupts and delays outside attempts to build Allies or Finance influence in the Shore. I'll be reaching out to Premier Eve Lockridge to get her backing on this. The Shore should be a matter for Carthian consensus, not a land grab. This isn't aggression toward the Invictus. Its buying time for the Movement saying: we decide together, and we haven't decided yet.

Characters Involved: Myself, and I'm seeking Premier Eve Lockridge's endorsement of the action

Skills and Powers Used: Picket Line (Carthian territory action)  will add contacts 5 to allies Finance 3 to block consolidation attempts to claim 4th and 5th Allies Finance dots. If I can't add them all to that, then Allies (politics) will get thrown into the blocking mix, ie APRA actions and regulatory review. Allies (finance) 3+2=5 and Allies (politics) 2+3 = 5, for two faceted blocking of any territory moves into the Shore (if required)

Desired Outcome: Block or impede non-Carthian attempts to consolidate Allies or Finance influence in the Shore for this downtime period, pending Carthian committee discussion on the territory's future. Secure the Premier's visible support to legitimise the action as a Movement decision, not a personal one.

XP Spent: None
responses.project_4_description:
Project Name: Building the Temple of Mammon

Project Description: Sydney is a new court, and feeding is one of the most dangerous things any Kindred does - it's where the Masquerade is thinnest and where neonates are most vulnerable. The Mammon cult is being established as a communal feeding ground and safe space for the court. I want to invest my time and resources into making this work i.e. helping set up the mortal-facing infrastructure, using my Finance contacts to ensure the operation is financially sustainable, and my Obfuscate to keep the whole thing discreet and secure from mortal attention. This is something that benefits every Kindred in Sydney regardless of covenant. Safe feeding means fewer Masquerade breaches, fewer desperate neonates making mistakes, and a court that actually functions. I want the Movement's fingerprints on this ... the Carthians helped build something that serves everyone, not just ourselves.

Characters Involved: Myself. Open to other Carthians or cross-covenant Kindred who want to contribute.

Skills and Powers Used: Manipulation + Finance (establishing sustainable mortal-facing operations), Wits + Subterfuge (keeping the operation discreet), Obfuscate (security - ensuring mortal attention doesn't land on the cult's activities)

Desired Outcome: Advance the establishment of the Mammon cult as a functional communal feeding resource for the court. Build cross-covenant goodwill by demonstrating Carthian commitment to shared infrastructure. Establish Yusuf as a visible contributor to something that serves the whole city.

XP Spent: None

Allies 3-  (Finance) ** MAYBE USED ALREADY in "CLOSED FOR BUSINESS"**
Project Description: Sydney is a new court and everyone is scrambling to establish mortal infrastructure. I run my own financial networks and I know how this game works, which means I know other Kindred are doing the same thing. I want my finance people to map the landscape: who is making significant financial moves in Sydney right now? New shell companies, unusual property acquisitions, sudden investment in specific industries or territories. Palleon International is one obvious name worth checking, but I doubt they're the only ones. The Lancea, the Crone, even other Carthians: anyone planting financial flags in Sydney will leave traces. I want a picture of the whole board, not just one piece.
Desired Outcome: A broad intelligence picture of significant mortal financial activity in Sydney that looks like it might be Kindred-adjacent: new corporate entities, unusual investment patterns, territory-by-territory financial footprint.

Allies 2 -  (Politics) ** MAYBE USED ALREADY in "CLOSED FOR BUSINESS" unless i can stick all Contacts dots into Finance **
Project Description: The Mammon cult needs a mortal-facing cover that holds up to scrutiny. I want my political contacts to help smooth the path, identifying what kind of community organisation, charitable trust, or religious entity would be the best legal vehicle for what we're building. What permits do we need, what zoning applies, who in local government would be sympathetic or at least indifferent? I also want them keeping an ear out for any mortal political figures who seem to be unusually responsive to specific interests. That's often a sign that another Kindred has gotten there first.
Desired Outcome: Clear the bureaucratic path for the Mammon cult's mortal-facing infrastructure. Flag any mortal political figures who appear to already be under Kindred influence.

Allies 2 -  (Underworld) 
Project Description: I used to run a smuggling ring before I was even Kindred. I know how the underworld works, and I know that mortal criminal networks are prime feeding grounds for Kindred influence: muscle, money laundering, logistics, intimidation. I want my underworld contacts to tell me what's shifting on the ground in Sydney. Are there criminal organisations that have recently changed behaviour? New discipline, new money, new leadership that doesn't quite make sense? That's the fingerprint of Kindred involvement. I'm particularly interested in whether anyone is consolidating underworld influence in territories the Movement cares about, but I want the full picture.
Desired Outcome: Map Kindred-adjacent underworld activity across Sydney. Identify criminal networks that show signs of supernatural patronage: sudden discipline, unexplained funding, leadership changes. Flag any encroachment on territories relevant to the Carthian Movement.

Contacts (Media)

Palleon International is one name worth a look, but more broadly, are there any companies or individuals making headlines, facing scrutiny, or generating buzz that might be worth paying attention to? New philanthropists nobody's heard of, businesses expanding suspiciously fast, executives who appeared from nowhere with impeccable credentials. Sometimes the mortal press does my homework for me.

Contacts (Occult)

I want to know what the occult-adjacent mortal community in Sydney looks like. What groups are already operating? Desired Outcome: A map of Sydney's mortal occult landscape: existing groups, territorial concentrations, potential allies or obstacles for establishing the Mammon cult. Flag anything that looks like it might already be under Kindred patronage.

Contacts (Police)

I want to know if there's anything on the police radar that could be a problem: unusual crime patterns, missing persons clusters, investigations into businesses or organisations that might be Kindred-adjacent. Contacts (High Society)
Project Description: High society is where mortal money meets mortal influence, and it's a natural hunting ground for Kindred, especially Invictus and Daeva. I want to know who's been making waves in Sydney's mortal elite recently. Any new faces at charity galas, board appointments, or social clubs that seem to have come from nowhere? Anyone spending conspicuously or building social capital fast? Kindred playing the high society game tend to leave a distinct footprint: too charming, too generous, too well-connected too quickly.
Desired Outcome: Intelligence on Sydney's mortal high society: new players, unusual social climbers, and any signs of supernatural patronage among the mortal elite.

responses.game_recount:
- When Bishop Judge interacted with Kirk, she immediately admonished him for perking up so loud and being so excited when he heard Mac’s name. Kirk took this in stride and genuflected immediately, and asked for the chance to undergo the Apocrypha of the Confessional. Kirk told Edna he was feeling low on Humanity; and that he could feel himself slipping away. She denied him the chance to amend this with a Gangrel Red Immersion, and also denied him the chance to submit to Conrad as his Regent through Vinculum to protect him from Kirk in Frenzy, saying she doesn’t know enough about Kirk or Conrad to judge them well yet. Despite this, Kirk likes her. She’s stern, impartial and fair, and something of a peacemaker. She said she’d help him with his Humanity problem, and while Kirk doesn’t actually think she can, it’s sweet that she’d try.

- Kirk was excited to meet Mac again, who almost hid behind Doc most of the evening in their interactions. He beamed at them both, showing his teeth in a wide grin anytime they were in eye-shot. With their history, Kirk felt almost like it was a piece of his old home had followed him to Sydney, and it was by no means the most unsavoury part. He liked Mac already, and thinks Doc is… integritous. But they bothered him, ribbed him and cajoled him constantly, and as he was on his best behaviour, he didn’t feel like he could return in kind. Doc was especially merciless. Now she is in charge of “Protecting Sydney”, he feels like he’s lost the chance to return the favour.

- Carver was largely gentlemanly to Kirk, and Kirk liked being called “Brother”; it made him feel accepted. That said, the Chronicler did have an awkward moment with Kirk, when he asked what Kirk would do if the Bishop asked him to attack and neutralise Conrad. Kirk extracted himself immediately from the conversation, and for several talking-toos from Conrad about that same fact. Carver also was exacting in his words about Kirk to his covenant. Kirk knows what he is, and knew it was true, but his seeming inadequacy being aired in front of the covenant coterie (especially the girls, yay subconscious systemic misogynistic patriarchal mindsets) made him uncomfortable. He is neutral on Carver personally, but respects him fully.

- Anichka approached Kirk, who didn’t immediately clock she was a Crone. They had a brief conversation, where she revealed to him she’d had a dream she’d seen him in a burning church with another figure holding a lantern and a sword between them. He was told by Conrad to push it from his mind, but it lingers. She also seems to know something about the Grimalkin, but she is either playing dumb, or her visions are not revealing as much to her as Kirk would like.

- Kirk immediately scared Ivana. He’s a massive, meatheaded bastard, and mean-mugged her when she walked over in cold silence. He was guarding the Premier and his Inquisitor at the time, but didn’t need to be such a dick to her, and realises that. He almost apologised, in his own way, especially when he found out she was Gangrel. He thinks she’s harmless, but also knows she’s a Crone.


Bonus:

Reed Justice antagonised Kirk, unprovoked, making fun of the way in which he self-regulated, mocking his “noises”. Kirk _really_ didn’t like that.

- Conrad’s sire’s sire, Keeper, got into Conrad’s face, and Kirk stood in the way partially, but didn’t want to ruin the relationship for Conrad, as there was clearly familiarity. Kirk could tell Conrad was upset when Keeper left, and made an effort to interpose his body between them the rest of the night.

- Kirk really, really liked Tegan. She even warned Kirk about the Carthian René, a threat he already was aware of, but appreciated it. She also went out of her way to help Kirk set up the potential of a Gangrel Immersion, and that meant a lot to the big lug. She’s the one person that Kirk trusts implicitly, besides Conrad.
responses.rp_shoutout: Arnold was lovely to finally RP with, and Ivana also was delightful, and sold wonderfully how nasty Kirk is.
responses.correspondence:
Dear Dad,

Damn you. I’m doing it. I’m a Paladin, in court in Sydney, making waves and I’m even being considered for positions in the City. I didn’t get none, probably because I wasn’t told about the Danse Macabre or anything about being a Gangrel by you, you sick, twisted heretic.

Conrad, the Inquisitor who stopped be from diablerising you, is Primogen. And I’m his Paladin. When the Lord of Our Lord returns, I’m going to be judged and my merits will be measured. I’m doing something right; I can hope to be unmade instead of suffer anymore. You never did, and you’ll burn. I hope you read this before you do, heretic.

I hope the Lorekeeper shoves this on your stake like a receipt spindle, 

Kirk (the one who got away)

Project description: Kirk’s Clan Status in Sydney surprised him (and me, DAMN!). Among the Gangrel, Kirk feels his first taste of deference and respect from his fellow Kindred from outside the Lance. While he’s locked in his mindset of religious subservience and self-loathing, the importance of this doesn’t escape him. He isn’t a manipulative Kindred, so he wouldn’t really know what to do about it, but would know something should be done. So, he jumps on Gumtree and buys some livestock. A lamb for his fellow Lance member Teagan, goats for the Crone Gangrel, and pigs for the Savage Carthians, and one-by-one, loads them into the back of his little hatchback (with a tarp down) and drives them to each of the Gangrel that he talked to in court. He suspects Doc won’t accept but offers anyway, more out of defiance than duty. He doesn’t want to be seen around the Crone at all, so it’s very much a drop and dash job, but he (badly) tied a ribbon to the goats horns, so they know it’s a gift.
Characters involved: All Gangrel at Court last session. I think the characters were Ivana, Tegan, Doc and… the new Harpy, who’se name escapes me.

responses.feeding_description:
On top of his Cult, Keeper feeds from all walks of life in all sorts of places. He has a taste for those he senses may be carrying trauma and often takes memories as well as blood. He cloaks himself in the Mantle of the Predator Goddess (Cruac 4 - SotC pg186) and studies people while under Face in the Crowd (Obfuscate 1), gliding through the city like the predator he is until he finds someone lucky enough to align with his current predilection and uses Mesmerize on them to make them compliant before feeding and removing the memory of the encounter from them like he was ever even there. The process of finding someone isn't quick and he doesn't hurry it, he has eternity, he spends more time feeding than most. If he does find someone particularly interesting, he may later work to absorbed them into his cult.

Base [Intelligence 4 + Empathy 4 (Spec: Sin-Eater) + Dominate (4)] + Mantle of the Predator Goddess (Cruac 4) = 16 Dice

responses.correspondence:
My dearest Odeliese, 

Soon I will find a home for you, and you shall enjoy all the beauty and wonder you deserve once more.

Things are so vibrant in Sydney, it is unlike any place we have ever been. The people here are untouched by my kind, and the world is different for it. I must remember it all before it changes, and uncover the memories lost this last short while we have not been able to tread here.

I so look forward to sharing these stories with you, I has been difficult without you, but you will be here soon.

Your love immortal,

Henry
